fields.description:
## Context / Why All code landing on `main` goes through Gerrit's two-vote gate (LLM-Review + Verified). We want every such commit associated with a rebar ticket, enforced as part of the **Verified (CI)** leg — the only enforcement point that both blocks submit AND can resolve an ID against the live store. A `rebar-ticket:` commit trailer is introduced by this work as the convention (it appears on recent commits but nothing parses it yet). CI does not apply to specialized branches (the `tickets` branch never goes through Gerrit), so this gates only code changes to `main`. The mechanism is intended to be reusable, so it is documented as a first-class rebar convention. ## What Add a `rebar verify-commit-ticket` check that extracts a ticket reference from a commit message and resolves it against the authoritative store via the standard config store logic (`config.tracker_dir()` / `config.tickets_branch()`), then wire it as a fail-fast job in `gerrit-verify.yaml` so a missing/unresolvable ticket drives Verified-1. Accept alias, full ID, short ID, and Jira key (project prefix) — all handled by the existing `resolve_ticket_id`. ## Decisions - **Location:** canonical is the `rebar-ticket: ` trailer; a leading `:` subject token is also accepted. First-element-ONLY enforcement is NOT adopted: no performance gain (a single store lookup regardless of position) and no fragility gain (both are fixed-position parses), while it would constrain every subject and eat the 50-char budget. - **Single source of truth:** the expected-format wording lives in ONE Python constant (`EXPECTED_FORMAT`). The CLI error and `--help` print it; the golden help capture and a doc-drift test pin `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` to it, so they cannot diverge. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py`: - `EXPECTED_FORMAT: str` — the shared expected-format/accepted-forms/example/fix text. - `extract_ticket_refs(message) -> list[str]` — pull candidates from the `rebar-ticket:` trailer (case-insensitive; value tokens split on whitespace and stripped of `()`,`,`) and from a leading `:` subject token. **SECURITY:** filter candidates to safe ID shapes only (full/short hex `^[a-z0-9]{4}(-[a-z0-9]{4}){1,3}$`, alias `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{2,}$`, Jira `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]+-[0-9]+$`) BEFORE any store lookup — a candidate like `../../etc/passwd` must be dropped, never passed to the resolver. - `verify_commit_message(message, *, root=None) -> (ok: bool, tried: list[str], detail)` — resolve each safe candidate via `resolve_ticket_id` against `config.tracker_dir(root)`; ok if ANY resolves. - [ ] CLI `rebar verify-commit-ticket` (intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`, mirroring the `review-plan` intercept). Input is ONE of `--rev ` (reads via `git show -s --format=%B`), `--message-file

`, or `--message ` (mutually exclusive; default `--rev HEAD`). Behavior: - `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` false → exit 0 (no-op / staged rollout). - Merge commit (>1 parent, when `--rev` given) → exit 0 (exempt). - A safe candidate resolves → exit 0. - Else exit 1, printing `EXPECTED_FORMAT`: the `rebar-ticket: ` trailer format, the leading `:` alternative, the accepted ID forms (alias/full/short/Jira `-`), a worked example, the candidate(s) tried (or `(none present)`) with "none resolved", and the fix (add the trailer, `git commit --amend`, re-push). - [ ] Subcommand registration (all four touchpoints, else CI contract tests fail): (a) the intercept arm in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`; (b) `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` (help text; `_help.py` LOADS these — `known_subcommands()` scans this dir, so the file's presence registers the command); (c) a `verify-commit-ticket` line in `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt`; (d) add `verify-commit-ticket` to the `individual` set in `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py`, and regenerate the 6 golden files `tests/golden/cli_help/verify-commit-ticket__{dashhelp,help}.{out,err,exit}` via the repo's golden-capture path. - [ ] Config: `verify.require_ticket_for_commit: bool = False` in `VerifyConfig` + `_SECTIONS['verify']` in `src/rebar/config.py` (env `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_TICKET_FOR_COMMIT` via the generic env layer); enabled for THIS project in `rebar.toml`. - [ ] Store resolution uses the standard config helpers `config.tracker_dir()` / `config.tickets_branch()` and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (relocates the store, highest precedence) / `tracker.dir` / `tracker.branch` — no bespoke store path. (Local resolution reads the mounted store files; the CI job separately mounts that store — see below.) - [ ] CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` gains a `require-ticket` job (needs `clear-vote`, and `build-and-test` + `vote` gain it in `needs` so a failure fails the run → Verified-1, fail-fast before the matrix): - checkout the Gerrit change (`checkout-gerrit-change-action`) + `pip install -e .`; - mount the authoritative tickets store from GitHub, **idempotently and with retry**: `git worktree prune`; `rm -rf .tickets-tracker`; fetch with a timeout and up to 3 attempts: `git fetch --depth=1 https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets`; `git worktree add --detach .tickets-tracker FETCH_HEAD`. On persistent fetch failure, print a DISTINGUISHABLE infra-error (not a missing-ticket error) telling the contributor to `recheck`, and fail closed (consistent with the two-vote fail-closed model); - run `rebar verify-commit-ticket --rev HEAD` (the CLI reads the message itself from git; no untrusted commit text is interpolated onto a shell command line). - [ ] Tests `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py`: - extraction: trailer with parenthetical, leading `:`, multiple, none, AND a path-traversal candidate is dropped (security); - resolution against a fixture store for full / short / alias / prefix, and a Jira key via a `.bridge_state/bindings.json` fixture; - config-gate off → exit 0; enabled + no ticket → exit 1 AND the diagnostic contains the trailer format + accepted forms + an example (asserted against `EXPECTED_FORMAT`); - merge-commit exempt; the three input modes; a valid leading-`:` commit → exit 0. - [ ] Doc-drift test: a unit test imports `EXPECTED_FORMAT` and asserts `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` contains its text, so docs cannot silently drift. - [ ] Docs (accurate + discoverable; a reusable, rebar-enforced convention for BOTH clients and contributors): - `CONTRIBUTING.md` — every commit to `main` must carry a resolvable ticket ref; the trailer format, the leading-`:` alternative, accepted ID forms, CI enforcement. - `CLAUDE.md` — in the `## Git workflow (code changes)` section, the same for agents. - `docs/config.md` — the `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` flag. - `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` (new) — the reusable mechanism: trailer convention + `rebar verify-commit-ticket` + extraction/resolution + config; linked from the two above. ## Verification - `pytest tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py -q` - `pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py -q` (registration parity) - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message "x: no ticket"; echo $?` → 1, printing EXPECTED_FORMAT - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message-file <(git show -s --format=%B HEAD); echo $?` → 0 ## Out of scope - Non-Gerrit paths (the `tickets` branch; direct GitHub pushes — already blocked by the ruleset). - An advisory client-side `commit-msg` hook (optional future nicety). - A `rebar-ticket: none` bypass sentinel (kept strict; merge commits are the only exemption).
